title: Sedum rochea
taxonomy: Sedum rochea is classified within the family Crassulaceae, commonly known as the stonecrop family. This family comprises over 1,400 species of succulent plants, many of which are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Sedum rochea is further classified as part of the genus Sedum, which includes around 600 species of succulent plants. The genus Sedum is divided into several sections, with Sedum rochea belonging to the section Sedum. Sedum rochea is a species of succulent plant native to the Himalayas and surrounding regions.

preparations: Sedum rochea is not a recognized species of Sedum, however, I can provide information on herbal preparations related to Sedum. intro: Sedum rochea, also known as Rocky Sedum or Mountain Sedum, is a succulent plant species native to the Himalayan region, characterized by its thick, fleshy leaves and stems. It possesses numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties, making it potentially useful in treating various ailments such as wound healing, fever reduction, and gastrointestinal issues. The bioactive constituents of Sedum rochea include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Sedum rochea is often prepared into herbal teas, decoctions, or extracts, which are used to treat a range of conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin infections.
